title: Modern Typing, Generics, and API Evolution · Mastery Check
summary: Type-check interchangeable repositories, policies, events, and domain collections in Python 3.14. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a statically checked domain API. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use Python 3.14 annotation syntax, built-in generics, unions, aliases, and forward references consistently.
Define generic classes and functions whose type parameters express a real input-output relationship.
Reason about covariance, contravariance, invariance, mutation, and substitutability in generic APIs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""python-oop cumulative stakeholder program."""

from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

# Add one tested, typed, inspectable program increment per activity.

steps.instruction:
Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: python generics typing. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Annotations are eagerly evaluated and runtime-enforced in every Python 3.14 program. This fails the Oop Modern Annotations contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: Dataclasses Documentation (Python 3.14.6) — Controls generated methods, fields, factories, frozen behavior, slots, and post-initialization.

content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use Python 3.14 annotation syntax, built-in generics, unions, aliases, and forward references consistently.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Oop Annotation Introspection applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.
